sections.title: What is Probability?
points: Probability shows the chance of choosing someone from a group.
The formula is: target count / total count.
Write the answer as a fraction or simplified ratio.

sections.title: Simple Probability
points: If Set A has 40 people out of 60 total, probability = 40/60.
Always count the right parts of the Venn diagram.

sections.title: Probability of 'Only'
points: For 'B only', use the part that is in B but not in A.
Example: If B only = 20 and total is 60, probability = 20/60 = 1/3.
Do not include the intersection in 'only' questions.

sections.title: Probability of Neither
points: If the outside region has 14 people and total is 60, probability = 14/60.
Always include outside when the question asks for neither.
Count all regions before writing the probability.

cards.fact: Chance = target count divided by total count.
cards.mnemonic: Probability compares one part to the whole.
The target is what we want to pick.

cards.fact: The number of all possible outcomes.
cards.mnemonic: Total is the full group size.
Neither means none of the options.
